title: The happy secret to better work | Shawn Achor

transcript.chinese: 当我七岁和我姐姐五岁的时候，我们在双层床上玩耍。
transcript.english: When I was seven years old and my sister was just five years old, we were playing on top of a bunk bed.

transcript.chinese: 当时我比我姐姐大两岁——我的意思是，我现在比她大两岁——但当时这意味着她必须做我想做的一切，而我想玩战争。
transcript.english: I was two years older than my sister at the time -- I mean, I'm two years older than her now -- but at the time it meant she had to do everything that I wanted to do, and I wanted to play war.

transcript.chinese: 所以我们躺在双层床上。
transcript.english: So we were up on top of our bunk beds.

transcript.chinese: 在双层床的一侧，我把我所有的退伍军人物品都拿出来了。
transcript.english: And on one side of the bunk bed, I had put out all of my G.I.

transcript.chinese: 乔士兵和武器装备。
transcript.english: Joe soldiers and weaponry.

transcript.chinese: 另一边，我姐姐的所有小马驹都做好了骑兵冲锋的准备。
transcript.english: And on the other side were all my sister's My Little Ponies ready for a cavalry charge.

transcript.chinese: 对于那天下午实际发生的事情有不同的说法，但由于我姐姐今天不在我们这里，让我告诉你真实的故事 - （笑声）这是我姐姐有点笨拙的一面。
transcript.english: There are differing accounts of what actually happened that afternoon, but since my sister is not here with us today, let me tell you the true story -- (Laughter) which is my sister's a little on the clumsy side.

transcript.chinese: 不知何故，在没有哥哥的任何帮助或推动的情况下，艾米从双层床的顶部消失了，然后摔在地板上。
transcript.english: Somehow, without any help or push from her older brother at all, Amy disappeared off of the top of the bunk bed and landed with this crash on the floor.

transcript.chinese: 我紧张地从床边往外看，想看看摔倒的妹妹发生了什么事，发现她痛苦地四肢着地，双手双膝着地。
transcript.english: I nervously peered over the side of the bed to see what had befallen my fallen sister and saw that she had landed painfully on her hands and knees on all fours on the ground.

transcript.chinese: 我很紧张，因为我的父母责成我确保我和姐姐尽可能安全、安静地玩耍。
transcript.english: I was nervous because my parents had charged me with making sure that my sister and I played as safely and as quietly as possible.

transcript.chinese: 就在一周前我不小心折断了艾米的手臂——（笑声）
transcript.english: And seeing as how I had accidentally broken Amy's arm just one week before -- (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: （笑声结束）英勇地把她从迎面而来的想象中的狙击手子弹中推开，（笑声）我还没有为此表示感谢，我已经尽了最大的努力——她甚至没有看到它的到来——我正在努力表现出最好的行为。
transcript.english: (Laughter ends) heroically pushing her out of the way of an oncoming imaginary sniper bullet, (Laughter) for which I have yet to be thanked, I was trying as hard as I could -- she didn't even see it coming -- I was trying hard to be on my best behavior.

transcript.chinese: 我看到了姐姐的脸，这痛苦、痛苦和惊讶的哀嚎威胁着要从她嘴里爆发出来，把我父母从他们已经习惯的漫长冬日小睡中吵醒。
transcript.english: And I saw my sister's face, this wail of pain and suffering and surprise threatening to erupt from her mouth and wake my parents from the long winter's nap for which they had settled.

transcript.chinese: 所以我做了我那疯狂的七岁大脑所能想到的唯一一件事来避免这场悲剧。
transcript.english: So I did the only thing my frantic seven year-old brain could think to do to avert this tragedy.

transcript.chinese: 如果你有孩子，你已经见过数百次这种情况了。
transcript.english: And if you have children, you've seen this hundreds of times.

transcript.chinese: 我说：“艾米，等等。
transcript.english: I said, "Amy, wait.

transcript.chinese: 别哭。
transcript.english: Don't cry.

transcript.chinese: 你看到你是怎么着陆的了吗？
transcript.english: Did you see how you landed?

transcript.chinese: 人类没有像这样四肢着地的。
transcript.english: No human lands on all fours like that.

transcript.chinese: 艾米，我想这意味着你是一只独角兽。” （笑声）现在，那是作弊，因为她不想成为受伤的五岁小妹妹艾米，而是成为特殊的独角兽艾米。
transcript.english: Amy, I think this means you're a unicorn." (Laughter) Now, that was cheating, because there was nothing she would want more than not to be Amy the hurt five year-old little sister, but Amy the special unicorn.

transcript.chinese: 当然，她的大脑在过去从未有过这种选择。
transcript.english: Of course, this option was open to her brain at no point in the past.

transcript.chinese: 你可以看到我可怜的、受操纵的妹妹如何面对冲突，因为她的小大脑试图投入资源来感受她刚刚经历的痛苦和惊喜，或者思考她作为独角兽的新身份。
transcript.english: And you could see how my poor, manipulated sister faced conflict, as her little brain attempted to devote resources to feeling the pain and suffering and surprise she just experienced, or contemplating her new-found identity as a unicorn.

transcript.chinese: 而后者获胜了。
transcript.english: And the latter won.

transcript.chinese: 她没有哭泣，也没有停止玩耍，也没有叫醒我的父母，以免给我带来所有负面后果，她脸上露出了微笑，像小独角兽一样优雅地爬回双层床上——（笑声）一条腿断了。
transcript.english: Instead of crying or ceasing our play, instead of waking my parents, with all the negative consequences for me, a smile spread across her face and she scrambled back up onto the bunk bed with all the grace of a baby unicorn -- (Laughter) with one broken leg.

transcript.chinese: 我们在五岁和七岁的年纪偶然发现的东西——当时我们并不知道——将成为二十年后我们看待人类大脑的科学革命的先锋。
transcript.english: What we stumbled across at this tender age of just five and seven -- we had no idea at the time -- was was going be at the vanguard of a scientific revolution occurring two decades later in the way that we look at the human brain.

transcript.chinese: 我们偶然发现了一种叫做积极心理学的东西，这就是我今天在这里的原因，也是我每天早上醒来的原因。
transcript.english: We had stumbled across something called positive psychology, which is the reason I'm here today and the reason that I wake up every morning.

transcript.chinese: 当我开始在学术界之外、公司和学校谈论这项研究时，他们说永远不要做的第一件事就是从图表开始。
transcript.english: When I started talking about this research outside of academia, with companies and schools, the first thing they said to never do is to start with a graph.

transcript.chinese: 我想做的第一件事是从图表开始。
transcript.english: The first thing I want to do is start with a graph.

transcript.chinese: 这张图看起来很无聊，但这就是我每天早上兴奋醒来的原因。
transcript.english: This graph looks boring, but it is the reason I get excited and wake up every morning.

transcript.chinese: 这张图根本没有任何意义；这是假数据。
transcript.english: And this graph doesn't even mean anything; it's fake data.

transcript.chinese: 我们发现的是——（笑声）如果我得到这些研究你的数据，我会很兴奋，因为那里有一种趋势，这意味着我可以发表，这才是真正重要的。
transcript.english: What we found is -- (Laughter) If I got this data studying you, I would be thrilled, because there's a trend there, and that means that I can get published, which is all that really matters.

transcript.chinese: 曲线上方有一个奇怪的红点，房间里有一个怪人——我知道你是谁，我早些时候见过你——这没问题。
transcript.english: There is one weird red dot above the curve, there's one weirdo in the room -- I know who you are, I saw you earlier -- that's no problem.

transcript.chinese: 正如你们大多数人所知，这没问题，因为我可以删除那个点。
transcript.english: That's no problem, as most of you know, because I can just delete that dot.

transcript.chinese: 我可以删除那个点，因为这显然是一个测量错误。
transcript.english: I can delete that dot because that's clearly a measurement error.

transcript.chinese: 我们知道这是一个测量错误，因为它弄乱了我的数据。
transcript.english: And we know that's a measurement error because it's messing up my data.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）因此，我们在经济学、统计学、商业和心理学课程中教给人们的第一件事就是如何以统计上有效的方式消除怪人。
transcript.english: (Laughter) So one of the first things we teach people in economics, statistics, business and psychology courses is how, in a statistically valid way, do we eliminate the weirdos.

transcript.chinese: 我们如何消除异常值以便找到最佳拟合线？
transcript.english: How do we eliminate the outliers so we can find the line of best fit?

transcript.chinese: 如果我想知道普通人应该服用多少 Advil（两片），那就太棒了。
transcript.english: Which is fantastic if I'm trying to find out how many Advil the average person should be taking -- two.

transcript.chinese: 但如果我对你的潜力感兴趣，或者对幸福、生产力、精力或创造力感兴趣，我们正在用科学创造普通人的崇拜。
transcript.english: But if I'm interested in your potential, or for happiness or productivity or energy or creativity, we're creating the cult of the average with science.

transcript.chinese: 如果我问这样的问题：“孩子在教室里学会阅读的速度有多快？”科学家们将答案改为“普通孩子在教室里学习阅读的速度有多快？”我们会根据平均水平调整班级。
transcript.english: If I asked a question like, "How fast can a child learn how to read in a classroom?" scientists change the answer to "How fast does the average child learn how to read in that classroom?" and we tailor the class towards the average.

transcript.chinese: 如果你的成绩低于平均水平，那么心理学家会很兴奋，因为这意味着你患有抑郁症或患有疾病，或者希望两者兼而有之。
transcript.english: If you fall below the average, then psychologists get thrilled, because that means you're depressed or have a disorder, or hopefully both.

transcript.chinese: 我们希望两者兼而有之，因为我们的商业模式是，如果您带着一个问题参加治疗，我们希望确保您离开时知道自己有十个问题，这样您就会不断回来。
transcript.english: We're hoping for both because our business model is, if you come into a therapy session with one problem, we want to make sure you leave knowing you have ten, so you keep coming back.

transcript.chinese: 如有必要，我们会回到你的童年，但最终我们想让你恢复正常。
transcript.english: We'll go back into your childhood if necessary, but eventually we want to make you normal again.

transcript.chinese: 但正常只是平均水平。
transcript.english: But normal is merely average.

transcript.chinese: 积极心理学认为，如果我们仅仅研究平均水平，我们就只能保持平均水平。
transcript.english: And positive psychology posits that if we study what is merely average, we will remain merely average.

transcript.chinese: 然后，我没有删除那些积极的异常值，而是有意做的是进入这样的人群并问，为什么？
transcript.english: Then instead of deleting those positive outliers, what I intentionally do is come into a population like this one and say, why?

transcript.chinese: 为什么你们中的一些人在智力、运动能力、音乐能力、创造力、精力水平、面对挑战的弹性、幽默感方面都高于曲线？
transcript.english: Why are some of you high above the curve in terms of intellectual, athletic, musical ability, creativity, energy levels, resiliency in the face of challenge, sense of humor?

transcript.chinese: 不管怎样，我不想删除你，而是想研究你。
transcript.english: Whatever it is, instead of deleting you, what I want to do is study you.

transcript.chinese: 因为也许我们可以收集信息，不仅仅是如何将人们提升到平均水平，而是将我们全球公司和学校的整体平均水平提高。
transcript.english: Because maybe we can glean information, not just how to move people up to the average, but move the entire average up in our companies and schools worldwide.

transcript.chinese: 这张图对我来说很重要的原因是，在新闻中，大多数信息都不是积极的。
transcript.english: The reason this graph is important to me is, on the news, the majority of the information is not positive.

transcript.chinese: 事实上它是负面的。
transcript.english: in fact it's negative.

transcript.chinese: 其中大部分是关于谋杀、腐败、疾病、自然灾害。
transcript.english: Most of it's about murder, corruption, diseases, natural disasters.

transcript.chinese: 很快，我的大脑开始认为这就是世界上消极与积极的准确比例。
transcript.english: And very quickly, my brain starts to think that's the accurate ratio of negative to positive in the world.

transcript.chinese: 这就造成了“医学院综合症”。在医学培训的第一年，当您阅读所有症状和疾病的清单时，突然您意识到自己患有所有这些症状和疾病。
transcript.english: This creates "the medical school syndrome." During the first year of medical training, as you read through a list of all the symptoms and diseases, suddenly you realize you have all of them.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我有一个姐夫，名叫波波，这是一个完全不同的故事。
transcript.english: (Laughter) I have a brother in-law named Bobo, which is a whole other story.

transcript.chinese: 波波与独角兽艾米结婚了。
transcript.english: Bobo married Amy the unicorn.

transcript.chinese: 波波从耶鲁医学院给我打电话（笑声），波波说：“肖恩，我得了麻风病。” （笑声）即使在耶鲁大学，这也是极其罕见的。
transcript.english: Bobo called me on the phone -- (Laughter) from Yale Medical School, and Bobo said, "Shawn, I have leprosy." (Laughter) Which, even at Yale, is extraordinarily rare.

transcript.chinese: 但我不知道如何安慰可怜的波波，因为他刚刚度过了整整一周的更年期。
transcript.english: But I had no idea how to console poor Bobo because he had just gotten over an entire week of menopause.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我们发现，塑造我们的不一定是现实，而是你的大脑看待世界的镜头塑造了你的现实。
transcript.english: (Laughter) We're finding it's not necessarily the reality that shapes us, but the lens through which your brain views the world that shapes your reality.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们能够改变视角，我们不仅可以改变您的幸福感，还可以同时改变每一项教育和商业成果。
transcript.english: And if we can change the lens, not only can we change your happiness, we can change every single educational and business outcome at the same time.

transcript.chinese: 我大胆地申请了哈佛。
transcript.english: I applied to Harvard on a dare.

transcript.chinese: 没想到能考上，家里也没钱上大学。
transcript.english: I didn't expect to get in, and my family had no money for college.

transcript.chinese: 两周后，当我获得军事奖学金时，他们让我走了。
transcript.english: When I got a military scholarship two weeks later, they let me go.

transcript.chinese: 一些原本不可能的事情变成了现实。
transcript.english: Something that wasn't even a possibility became a reality.

transcript.chinese: 我以为那里的每个人都会认为这是一种特权，他们会很高兴能在那里。
transcript.english: I assumed everyone there would see it as a privilege as well, that they'd be excited to be there.

transcript.chinese: 即使教室里挤满了比你聪明的人，我觉得只要在那个教室里你就会很高兴。
transcript.english: Even in a classroom full of people smarter than you, I felt you'd be happy just to be in that classroom.

transcript.chinese: 但我发现，虽然有些人经历过这样的情况，但当我四年后毕业，然后在接下来的八年里和学生们一起住在宿舍里时——哈佛要求我这样做；我不是那个人。
transcript.english: But what I found is, while some people experience that, when I graduated after my four years and then spent the next eight years living in the dorms with the students -- Harvard asked me to; I wasn't that guy.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我是一名官员，负责辅导学生度过困难的四年。
transcript.english: (Laughter) I was an officer to counsel students through the difficult four years.

transcript.chinese: 在我的研究和教学中，我发现这些学生，无论他们最初成功进入学校多么高兴，两周后，他们的大脑不再关注在那里的特权，也不是他们的哲学或物理，而是竞争，工作量，
transcript.english: And in my research and my teaching, I found that these students, no matter how happy they were with their original success of getting into the school, two weeks later their brains were focused, not on the privilege of being there, nor on their philosophy or physics, but on the competition, the workload,

transcript.chinese: 麻烦、压力、抱怨。
transcript.english: the hassles, stresses, complaints.

transcript.chinese: 当我第一次进去时，我走进了新生食堂，这是我来自德克萨斯州韦科的朋友们的地方，那里是我长大的地方——我知道你们中的一些人知道这一点。
transcript.english: When I first went in there, I walked into the freshmen dining hall, which is where my friends from Waco, Texas, which is where I grew up -- I know some of you know this.

transcript.chinese: 当他们参观时，他们会环顾四周，然后说：“这个餐厅看起来就像霍格沃茨的餐厅。”确实如此，因为那是霍格沃茨的，那是哈佛的。
transcript.english: When they'd visit, they'd look around, and say, "This dining hall looks like something out of Hogwart's." It does, because that was Hogwart's and that's Harvard.

transcript.chinese: 当他们看到这一点时，他们说：“你为什么要浪费时间在哈佛研究幸福呢？
transcript.english: And when they see this, they say, "Why do you waste your time studying happiness at Harvard?

transcript.chinese: 哈佛学生可能会因为什么而感到不快乐？”这个问题是理解幸福科学的关键。
transcript.english: What does a Harvard student possibly have to be unhappy about?" Embedded within that question is the key to understanding the science of happiness.

transcript.chinese: 因为这个问题假设我们的外部世界可以预测我们的幸福水平，而实际上，如果我了解你外部世界的一切，我只能预测你 10% 的长期幸福。
transcript.english: Because what that question assumes is that our external world is predictive of our happiness levels, when in reality, if I know everything about your external world, I can only predict 10% of your long-term happiness.

transcript.chinese: 你 90% 的长期幸福不是由外部世界预测的，而是由你的大脑处理世界的方式预测的。
transcript.english: 90 percent of your long-term happiness is predicted not by the external world, but by the way your brain processes the world.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们改变的话如果我们改变幸福和成功的公式，我们就能改变影响现实的方式
transcript.english: And if we change it, if we change our formula for happiness and success, we can change the way that we can then affect reality.

transcript.chinese: 我们发现，只有 25% 的工作成功是由智商预测的，75% 的工作成功是由你的乐观程度、你的社会支持以及你将压力视为挑战而不是威胁的能力来预测的。
transcript.english: What we found is that only 25% of job successes are predicted by IQ, 75 percent of job successes are predicted by your optimism levels, your social support and your ability to see stress as a challenge instead of as a threat.

transcript.chinese: 因此，每年我们不仅会教导学生，还会举办健康周。
transcript.english: I talked to a New England boarding school, probably the most prestigious one, and they said, "We already know that.

transcript.chinese: 我们非常兴奋。
transcript.english: So every year, instead of just teaching our students, we have a wellness week.

transcript.chinese: 周一晚上，我们将邀请世界领先的专家谈论青少年抑郁症。
transcript.english: And we're so excited.

transcript.chinese: 周二晚上是校园暴力和欺凌事件。
transcript.english: Monday night we have the world's leading expert will speak about adolescent depression.

transcript.chinese: 周三晚上是饮食失调的日子。
transcript.english: Tuesday night it's school violence and bullying.

transcript.chinese: 周四晚上是非法吸毒的日子。
transcript.english: Wednesday night is eating disorders.

transcript.chinese: 周五晚上，我们要在危险的性行为和幸福之间做出选择。”（笑声）我说，“那是大多数人的周五晚上。”（笑声）（掌声）我很高兴你喜欢，但他们根本不喜欢这样。
transcript.english: Thursday night is illicit drug use.

transcript.chinese: 电话里一片沉默。
transcript.english: And Friday night we're trying to decide between risky sex or happiness." (Laughter) I said, "That's most people's Friday nights." (Laughter) (Applause) Which I'm glad you liked, but they did not like that at all.

transcript.chinese: 在沉默中，我说，“我很乐意在你们学校演讲，但这不是健康周，而是疾病周。
transcript.english: Silence on the phone.

transcript.chinese: 你概述了可能发生的所有消极的事情，但没有谈论积极的事情。”没有疾病并不代表健康。
transcript.english: And into the silence, I said, "I'd be happy to speak at your school, but that's not a wellness week, that's a sickness week.

transcript.chinese: 以下是我们如何获得健康：我们需要扭转幸福和成功的公式。
transcript.english: You've outlined all the negative things that can happen, but not talked about the positive." The absence of disease is not health.

transcript.chinese: 在过去的三年里，我走遍了 45 个国家，在经济低迷时期与学校和公司合作。
transcript.english: Here's how we get to health: We need to reverse the formula for happiness and success.

transcript.chinese: 我发现大多数公司和学校都遵循一个成功的公式，那就是：如果我更加努力，我就会更加努力 successful.
transcript.english: In the last three years, I've traveled to 45 countries, working with schools and companies in the midst of an economic downturn.

transcript.chinese: And if I'm more successful, then I'll be happier.
transcript.english: And I found that most companies and schools follow a formula for success, which is this: If I work harder, I'll be more successful.

transcript.chinese: That undergirds most of our parenting and managing styles, the way that we motivate our behavior.
transcript.english: And if I'm more successful, then I'll be happier.

transcript.chinese: And the problem is it's scientifically broken and backwards for two reasons.
transcript.english: That undergirds most of our parenting and managing styles, the way that we motivate our behavior.

transcript.chinese: Every time your brain has a success, you just changed the goalpost of what success looked like.
transcript.english: And the problem is it's scientifically broken and backwards for two reasons.

transcript.chinese: You got good grades, now you have to get better grades, you got into a good school and after you get into a better one, you got a good job, now you have to get a better job, you hit your sales target, we're going to change it.
transcript.english: Every time your brain has a success, you just changed the goalpost of what success looked like.

transcript.chinese: And if happiness is on the opposite side of success, your brain never gets there.
transcript.english: You got good grades, now you have to get better grades, you got into a good school and after you get into a better one, you got a good job, now you have to get a better job, you hit your sales target, we're going to change it.

transcript.chinese: We've pushed happiness over the cognitive horizon, as a society.
transcript.english: And if happiness is on the opposite side of success, your brain never gets there.

transcript.chinese: And that's because we think we have to be successful, then we'll be happier.
transcript.english: We've pushed happiness over the cognitive horizon, as a society.

transcript.chinese: But our brains work in the opposite order.
transcript.english: And that's because we think we have to be successful, then we'll be happier.

transcript.chinese: If you can raise某人当前的积极性水平，那么他们的大脑就会体验到我们现在所说的幸福优势，即你的大脑在积极的情况下表现得更好，你的创造力会提高，你的能量水平会提高
transcript.english: But our brains work in the opposite order.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，你的大脑在积极的情况下比你的大脑在消极的情况下要高出37%。当积极而不是消极、中性或压力时，医生会更快、更准确地做出正确的诊断，这意味着我们可以逆转这个公式，那么我们的大脑就会更加成功，因为我们能够更加努力、更快、更聪明地工作，这样我们就可以开始了解我们的大脑实际上有什么能力。当你积极时，这个系统有两个功能，它不仅能让你更快乐，还能开启你大脑中的所有学习中心，让你以不同的方式适应世界。我们发现有一些方法可以训练你的大脑变得更加积极。
transcript.english: If you can raise somebody's level of positivity in the present, then their brain experiences what we now call a happiness advantage, which is your brain at positive performs significantly better than at negative, neutral or stressed.

transcript.chinese: 你的智力提高了，你的创造力提高了，你的能量水平提高了。
transcript.english: Your intelligence rises, your creativity rises, your energy levels rise.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我们发现每一项业务成果都得到了改善。
transcript.english: In fact, we've found that every single business outcome improves.

transcript.chinese: 处于积极状态的大脑比处于消极、中性或压力状态时的大脑生产力高出 31%。
transcript.english: Your brain at positive is 31% more productive than your brain at negative, neutral or stressed.

transcript.chinese: 您的销售业绩提高了 37%。
transcript.english: You're 37% better at sales.

transcript.chinese: 当呈阳性而不是呈阴性、中性或有压力时，医生做出正确诊断的速度要快 19%，准确率要高 19%。
transcript.english: Doctors are 19 percent faster, more accurate at coming up with the correct diagnosis when positive instead of negative, neutral or stressed.

transcript.chinese: 这意味着我们可以反转公式。
transcript.english: Which means we can reverse the formula.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们能找到一种让当下变得积极的方法，那么我们的大脑就会更加成功地工作，因为我们能够更加努力、更快、更聪明地工作。
transcript.english: If we can find a way of becoming positive in the present, then our brains work even more successfully as we're able to work harder, faster and more intelligently.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要能够扭转这个公式，这样我们才能开始了解我们的大脑实际上有什么能力。
transcript.english: We need to be able to reverse this formula so we can start to see what our brains are actually capable of.

transcript.chinese: 因为当您呈阳性时，多巴胺会涌入您的系统，它有两个功能。
transcript.english: Because dopamine, which floods into your system when you're positive, has two functions.

transcript.chinese: 它不仅让你更快乐，还能开启你大脑中的所有学习中心，让你以不同的方式适应世界。
transcript.english: Not only does it make you happier, it turns on all of the learning centers in your brain allowing you to adapt to the world in a different way.

transcript.chinese: 我们发现有一些方法可以训练你的大脑变得更加积极。
transcript.english: We've found there are ways that you can train your brain to be able to become more positive.

transcript.chinese: 在连续 21 天的短短两分钟时间内，我们实际上可以重新连接您的大脑，让您的大脑实际上更乐观、更成功地工作。
transcript.english: In just a two-minute span of time done for 21 days in a row, we can actually rewire your brain, allowing your brain to actually work more optimistically and more successfully.

transcript.chinese: 我们现在在我合作过的每家公司都做了这些研究，让他们连续 21 天写下三件让他们感激的新事情，每天三件新事情。
transcript.english: We've done these things in research now in every company that I've worked with, getting them to write down three new things that they're grateful for for 21 days in a row, three new things each day.

transcript.chinese: 最后，他们的大脑开始保留一种扫描世界的模式，首先不是寻找消极的一面，而是寻找积极的一面。
transcript.english: And at the end of that, their brain starts to retain a pattern of scanning the world not for the negative, but for the positive first.

transcript.chinese: 记录过去 24 小时内的一次积极经历可以让您的大脑重温它。
transcript.english: Journaling about one positive experience you've had over the past 24 hours allows your brain to relive it.

transcript.chinese: 锻炼会让你的大脑知道你的行为很重要。
transcript.english: Exercise teaches your brain that your behavior matters.

transcript.chinese: 我们发现冥想可以让你的大脑克服我们因试图同时完成多项任务而造成的文化多动症，并让我们的大脑专注于手头的任务。
transcript.english: We find that meditation allows your brain to get over the cultural ADHD that we've been creating by trying to do multiple tasks at once and allows our brains to focus on the task at hand.

transcript.chinese: 最后，随机的善意行为是有意识的善意行为。
transcript.english: And finally, random acts of kindness are conscious acts of kindness.

transcript.chinese: 当人们打开收件箱时，我们会让他们写一封积极的电子邮件，赞扬或感谢他们支持网络中的某人。
transcript.english: We get people, when they open up their inbox, to write one positive email praising or thanking somebody in their support network.

transcript.chinese: 通过进行这些活动并像训练我们的身体一样训练您的大脑，我们发现我们可以扭转幸福和成功的公式，这样做不仅会产生积极的涟漪，而且会带来真正的革命。
transcript.english: And by doing these activities and by training your brain just like we train our bodies, what we've found is we can reverse the formula for happiness and success, and in doing so, not only create ripples of positivity, but a real revolution.

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: Thank you very much.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
